title: Kimi K3: The open-weights escalation
excerpt: The global implications on the AI ecosystem.
content_html:
On Thursday July 16th, Moonshot AI released their latest flagship model Kimi K3. K3 is a 2.8T parameter MoE model which will have its weights released on July 27th. Much of this article follows as a reflection on the state of the ecosystem, under the assumption that Moonshot keeps their promise of the weights release date. This is a more extreme view of the equilibrium, and many of the results end up in a middle ground if the state of affairs is that China has similarly powerful, but closed models (i.e. K3 is never released).

The key fact is that either the open-to-closed or American-to-Chinese model performance gap has been reduced from the debated 6-9 months to something shorter, say 3-5 months.

From the release materials, it is clear that K3 is a true frontier model. It will be the closest open models have been to the frontier since DeepSeek R1. DeepSeek R1 was a different story. This was a Chinese lab being extremely quick to pivot to reasoning models and release one faster than many American companies. Kimi K3 an example of a Chinese lab executing on scaling the known areas: data, algorithms, architecture, tools, environments, etc.

Kimi K3 comes in at #2 overall on the Vals AI index, #3 overall on Artificial Analysis’s Intelligence Index (only beaten by Claude Fable and GPT-5.6 Sol Max while being cheaper), #1 overall in Frontend Code Arena, and more impressive results. Moonshot AI is going toe to toe with Anthropic and OpenAI with far, far fewer resources.

It is clearly the strongest open model ever released. It should be clear looking at this model that if adversarial distillation from the closed frontier models in the U.S. contributed, it is at most to a relatively small degree. AI observers who followed the distillation panic and came away with the wrong conclusion that Chinese AI labs are only producing good models due to IP theft are in for an awakening – that Chinese companies are extremely good at building models in the same way the leading American companies are. Moonshot AI is solving many of the same problems that folks at OpenAI or Anthropic are solving. I’m confident there will be more distillation discussion, and pressure, but the evidence is now out that Chinese companies can do more than just fast following.

Meeting some of the core Kimi team on my trip to China, it was clear to me that they had incredible culture, some would say aura, and a freedom to express it – within the constraints of a GPU-limited environment. Where building models is so much of a scaling game, much of the ability to build a good model still comes down individual execution, motivation, and expression. Having visited them, this result is less surprising. Having visited many AI companies, very few have a culture that you can immediately pick up like this.

At the same time, China’s AI adoption trends started later than those in the U.S. So, while all the Chinese labs have way less compute than their counterparts in the U.S., more of it can certainly go to training. When I joked around about how much compute an average researcher at OpenAI could have – say a few thousand H100 equivalent machines – the researchers at Kimi were shocked. The org chart and approach to building the Kimi models surely reflect this, but it is difficult to tease out what this looks like without substantial proprietary information.

The state of affairs on peak model performance is roughly as follows:

  1. Anthropic – Claude Fable 5

  2. OpenAI – GPT 5.6 Sol

  3. Moonshot AI – Kimi K3 (open weights*)

  4. SpaceXAI – Grok 4.5

  5. Zhipu (Z.ai) – GLM 5.2 (open weights)

  6. Meta – Muse Spark 1.1

  7. DeepMind – Gemini Flash 3.5

  8. Alibaba – Qwen 3.7 Max (3.8 announced, also to be open-weights, when writing)

It is astonishing to see DeepMind, and some of the other American giants this low. In many ways, the X AI team deserves more credit. A visual summary from Artificial Analysis is below:

This release and other recent events have caused a major change in direction for the most likely outcomes in the balance between open and closed models. I’ll unpack them individually.

In many ways, it feels like the start of a new era. An era with much more competition, but also a much higher need for coordination, as we rollout incredibly powerful technologies around the world.

Share

Many people started following China’s AI scene relatively recently, so they can reach the conclusion that releasing models openly is their core strategy. In fact, I think most labs have a core strategy far closer to Anthropic or OpenAI – build the best intelligence possible. Having followed and engaged with the Chinese labs for years now, the best explanation for their original turn to releasing their models openly is practicality. They needed to release the models openly to get adoption, attention, and feedback (especially in the high-value, Bay Area market).

For a long time, there had been very limited policy in China explaining the role of open-source AI, and what could be the “country-level strategy.” To my knowledge, no senior leaders had commented on open-source AI publicly. This changed this week too, as Xi Jinping gave a keynote address at the World AI Conference (WAIC), and very directly committed the future of China’s AI ecosystem to open-source and global diffusion. This commitment to the status quo, the same week as the announcement of the strongest open-weight model to date, is a clear mark in the early history of modern AI.

This comes during a time period where many potential paths forward have been discussed for the Chinese AI industry – Will they stay open? Can they keep up with the American labs in scaling? Is there a growing revenue market in China? With these, the focus has been on China’s risk tolerance, the companies’ ability to monetize, and any closely related reason for a company to stop releasing their best models openly.

In tying Xi’s commitment in time to a very strong model, China has implicitly commented on its risk tolerance with respect to releasing open-weight models. For the time being, it is a read into the perceived risks of topics like strong cybersecurity capabilities (or bio-dangers) within the Chinese system.

The simplest explanation is that China’s government is definitely following potential risks from the models closely – likely with more technical scope than the US government’s vibe regulation – and would take action if it measured risk. The simple explanation is that they do not find current frontier models to have meaningful risk.

At the same time, China’s economic decision makers think having AI adoption is good, so they can make profits on the industry later – after growing distribution (as China has done for cars, solar, advanced manufacturing, and many areas in recent history).

These can seem somewhat shocking, in an American AI media landscape that has gone through months of hype and fearmongering over the Claude Mythos model. This surprise should be excellent grounding – the world does not have a unanimous agreement with the narratives about AI that we hear most in the U.S.

Many of the narrators guiding the discussion on AI have clear incentives to depress the perceived capabilities of the best, open AI models. Dean Ball – who is personally supportive of open models, but now works at OpenAI – had a widely commented on post with some reflections on Kimi, where he said the following on open models. It is important to understand the statement, as it focuses the role of open models in the economic side of the AI buildout. Dean says:1

  1. Open-weight models are inherently decelerationist, and I’m continually surprised to see the so-called “accelerationists” so excited about open-weight models.

Explaining why open-weight models are a form of decelerationism is important to understanding the coming world order. He is right.

Open models are decelerationist economically for the frontier labs, which will slow the net investment and capex rollout for AI. This is due to the fact that strong open-weight AI models massively reduce the margin potential for the closed labs. This has two effects. First, the AI labs have fewer profits to re-invest into future models. Second, the market sees the terminal value of these companies as being lower, so they will kneecap future fundraising rounds. These together will slow timelines to the most transformative AI models, but I do not see them as strong enough effects to stop OpenAI and Anthropic from being a few of the top valued companies in the world.

These, to me, are a net good for society. As open-weight models are accelerationist for AI diffusion across the economy by having the entry price for intelligence at a certain level of performance be lower. Open models also encourage customization. The thing is that this type of diffusion is by its nature far slower than the frontier AI labs products, who sell tools used directly by developers. The potential for open models is for nearly every business to use them to craft domain-specific agents. This economic diffusion takes an extremely long time! I’ve described this as open-weight models being on a much slower starting, but potentially bigger exponential. The problem is, if closed models get too far ahead in raw capabilities, this ability to customize can be moot.

The combination of increased diffusion and decreased concentration of power in the AI labs I see to be very positive for the AI transition. It gives us more time to figure out the hard problems of new capabilities and lets more stakeholders impact the story – any one company is very likely to have issues with controlling the world’s most important technology safely.

It is, of course, important to me in this world for the best models to still be made by the U.S. companies, which will allow the US to control the trajectory of the technology and its values. I also expect this to be the case, as the U.S. has larger capital markets that are willing to invest in AI (and a growing share of profits), but it is not a given.

Kimi’s launch blog has some technical details that confirm the sort of improvements that are supplying the consistent model improvements we feel. To select one:

Kimi K3 is built on Kimi Delta Attention (KDA) and Attention Residuals (AttnRes), two architectural updates designed to improve how information flows across sequence length and model depth. We have also scaled up Mixture of Experts (MoE) sparsity, effectively activating 16 out of 896 experts when paired with a Stable LatentMoE framework. Together with refined training and data recipes, these structural changes yield an approximate 2.5× improvement in overall scaling efficiency compared to Kimi K2, allowing the model to convert compute into intelligence more effectively.

Training efficiency really adds up. They will result in continued, incredible steps for the models.

As an aside, tracing the path of this particular innovation through the ecosystem is an interesting example. Kimi Delta Attention (KDA) was introduced in the Kimi Linear paper, which is similar to the Gated DeltaNet used for Olmo Hybrid (my last Olmo model while at Ai2). Qwen’s latest models switched to a related architecture and the recent Nemotron models also are hybrid (but still closer to Mamba than Gated DeltaNet). It’s awesome to see new architecture ideas like these, which were heavily progressed by academia, get so quickly translated into frontier-scale models. Gated Delta Networks were introduced in late 2024, building on ideas from Mamba. By mid 2026, they’re in frontier models.

I chose to focus on this example, partially because the Kimi team put a cool number to innovations between models, but primarily to give space to a broader discussion of China’s resource efficiency.

It is becoming clear that the Chinese labs are far more capital efficient. In a world where scaling laws dictate that intelligence is proportional to effective capital – which buys compute, data, & talent – that may be the greatest strength your AI industry could ever have. There are many possible explanations for why this is the case, such as Chinese researchers being paid less while being more effective at LLM research puzzles, but we will probably never get such specific reasons.

Since writing my notes on China, I’m hearing more about an emerging data industry in China (far behind the billion dollar budgets of Anthropic for data) and that Chinese labs have access to meaningful training compute (by skirting export controls). Chinese companies do not have the same inference demand (until recently, as Moonshot AI had to pause new subscriptions for access to their K3 model - while the API is still live), so much more of their compute could go to training. These areas impinge heavily on the truth of the ability of the labs, but we have very limited measurement into them.

The facts on the ground are that these Chinese labs have raised orders of magnitude less capital than any slice of the American AI ecosystem. The most direct comparisons are to OpenAI and Anthropic, who have slightly better public models. Others, such as Google and Meta have the largest cash flows in the history of business, and are behind on building models. As for American neolabs, the picture is even more competitive – Thinking Machines released their first model recently, Inkling, which is strong but not in the same class as Kimi K3.

These American companies with more resources could still catch up, but you need to strongly weigh the public measurements we have of model quality and not resort to hope – which often reflects a bias. If the Chinese labs do have a latent advantage, they could continue to utilize that to build even stronger models than all the competitors! Many outcomes are plausible and K3 should increase most people’s probability that China can outright lead in AI capabilities in the near future on the back of more efficient training efforts – even if it’s not your most likely predicted outcome.

A big contributor to the capital efficiency is likely in the approach, where American labs are spending meaningful energy in pushing the frontier in dramatic, big steps, and the Chinese labs are more focused on catching up — this catch-up is cheaper. Just as the student model can outperform the teacher in distillation generally (not limited to the adversarial distillation of the Chinese labs), an approach of “trying to catch up” rather than “invent the next paradigm” could lead to stronger models.

The weekend after the Kimi K3 release, while writing this and discussing the events broadly, Alibaba announced that a 2.4 trillion parameter Qwen 3.8 model is coming soon with open-weights. Historically, Alibaba has kept their largest models as API-only offerings via their cloud business, so this is another big vibe shift opening the doors to the next chapter of the open model economy. Even if the model is behind Kimi K3 on benchmarks, it signifies that Chinese companies may not only be maintaining the status quo for their open model strategy, but leaning further into it.

If this Qwen 3.8 model releases soon, i.e. before the next Gemini model, it could push Google to the 8th position on the leaderboard of labs with the smartest models – a list that China has been climbing. There are other rumors of more strong Chinese models soon, with DeepSeek V4 expected to graduate out of it’s “preview” version.

Leave a comment

I think that if Claude Mythos was released as an open-weight model today, the negative outcomes would be relatively minor. This is a somewhat challenging opinion to hold, as we have very limited public cybersecurity evaluations and it is a complicated ecosystem (and because I trust many people at Anthropic). I still stand by it. The risks have been over-hyped.

The problem is that this will not always be the case for the strongest AI models. Far stronger models are coming — and with them increased risks — so it is an incredibly safe equilibrium for the best models to be accessed in a controlled, closed manner several months ahead of similar open-weight models.

Open-weight models which are very controllable by the user will always be coming — you cannot effectively ban digital products, especially from bad actors — as AI training has proven globally accessible longer than many analysts expected.

Still, as I write this, the government continues to flirt with more measures aimed at restricting open-weight models in the U.S. The latest is from Axios:

Behind the scenes: The Commerce Department last year considered adding multiple Chinese AI labs to its “Entity List,” which would effectively cut off U.S. access without a license, a source close to the administration told Axios.

  • The National Security Agency and White House Office of the National Cyber Director also considered putting out an advisory on Chinese AI lab threats last year, practically discouraging U.S. companies from using their tech, the source said.

  • The White House considered implementing an executive order saying U.S. companies could only host Chinese models if they could guarantee security and take liability if it were breached, the source added.

  • Commerce last summer also circulated draft rules within the administration leveraging its authorities to secure domestic supply chains to target Chinese open-source models, another source close to the administration said.

This would leave the U.S. in a very asymmetric state where the best models in the U.S. have guardrails on cybersecurity tasks, but global actors have access to great Chinese open-weight models to probe our defenses. This is one of many examples where banning open-weight models is not only harms the free markets of AI but also makes the ecosystem less safe in the short-term. There are other very bad outcomes, such as slowing the diffusion of AI applications and AI research, as I discussed above.

These equilibriums are very hard to maintain, especially as AI tools accelerate progress in the models, but it is important to maintain this status quo between open and closed. Having a model that is truly alone at the frontier in capabilities — something like Mythos when it was announced — also be open-weight poses serious risks as we go into the unknown of capabilities. Models are going to progress very fast and it is increasingly hard to measure their total capabilities.

We are then stuck in a world where we are trying to thread the needle on open models. It’s reasonable to not want something so powerful to be diffused globally in an instant, but meanwhile the makers of the models are incentivized to hype their capabilities, and their competitors are incentivized to hype their risks. It all comes down to careful measurement and proactive hardening of society to risk vectors.

This careening train of policy debates, model releases, and raucous reactions is only going to continue from today. We’ve been on a train of rapid progress, where all the key ideas of how AI should play out are tested, since the release of Claude Opus 4.5 last December, which sent us down the agentic pathway. The key to making good decisions here is evaluation capabilities, independent of the companies with the largest financial stakes. One of many actions needed then, as we enter the AGI era of AI governance, is an Operation Warp Speed style approach of bootstrapping state capacity (and other independent actors) that can evaluate models accurately, and study emerging risks.

Open-weight models, by accelerating the diffusion of capabilities, are a massive escalation in the good and the potential bad of AI. For now, the bad side of frontier language models has been largely hypothetical, but that will not always remain the case.

Having open weight models be slightly behind the closed frontier is our natural buffer to mitigate the risks. The key point is that we must collectively act to mitigate potential harms as they appear, and whether open-weight models are 3 or 6 or 9 months behind, that is still a very short timeline. If we regulate open-weight models heavy-handedly, I suspect much of the world will be lulled into thinking we no longer need to act. All we would’ve done is slightly delayed the inevitable — open models will continue to cross all the key capability thresholds eventually and regardless of legality.

Understanding and benefiting from this open-closed dance must be a collective action from the AI community across all sectors of power and influence over the coming years.

Kimi K3 is a watershed moment because frontier open-weight models are now real. Many hypotheses will be tested on where risks of open-weight models truly land — I suspect it’ll be narrower than many expect, and many risks of AI will still be proliferated by closed and “safer” APIs. The evaluation of these risks will evolve in time with an acceleration of AI’s integration in our economy. We cannot get one without the other, and we will continue to get both.

With this, 2025 was when open models started to be taken more seriously — especially when China leaped ahead with such a clear lead — as people realized that it would not be a unipolar world, with only American, closed AI labs determining the trajectory. 2026 is when those previously discussed, potential risks and accelerations due to truly frontier, open-weight models landed.

1

A large portion of the response was for the fourth bullet, which compared the inevitable outcome of open models to AI communism, which I think missed the mark. Specifically, the use of the word communism without explanation caused much of the blowback.

This is a somewhat challenging opinion to hold, as we have very limited public cybersecurity evaluations and it is a complicated ecosystem (and because I trust many people at Anthropic). I still stand by it. The risks have been over-hyped.The problem is that this will not always be the case for the strongest AI models. Far stronger models are coming — and with them increased risks — so it is an incredibly safe equilibrium for the best models to be accessed in a controlled, closed manner several months ahead of similar open-weight models. Open-weight models which are very controllable by the user will always be coming — you cannot effectively ban digital products, especially from bad actors — as AI training has proven globally accessible longer than many analysts expected.Still, as I write this, the government continues to flirt with more measures aimed at restricting open-weight models in the U.S. The latest is from Axios:Behind the scenes: The Commerce Department last year considered adding multiple Chinese AI labs to its “Entity List,” which would effectively cut off U.S. access without a license, a source close to the administration told Axios.The National Security Agency and White House Office of the National Cyber Director also considered putting out an advisory on Chinese AI lab threats last year, practically discouraging U.S. companies from using their tech, the source said.The White House considered implementing an executive order saying U.S. companies could only host Chinese models if they could guarantee security and take liability if it were breached, the source added.Commerce last summer also circulated draft rules within the administration leveraging its authorities to secure domestic supply chains to target Chinese open-source models, another source close to the administration said.This would leave the U.S. in a very asymmetric state where the best models in the U.S. have guardrails on cybersecurity tasks, but global actors have access to great Chinese open-weight models to probe our defenses. This is one of many examples where banning open-weight models is not only harms the free markets of AI but also makes the ecosystem less safe in the short-term. There are other very bad outcomes, such as slowing the diffusion of AI applications and AI research, as I discussed above. These equilibriums are very hard to maintain, especially as AI tools accelerate progress in the models, but it is important to maintain this status quo between open and closed. Having a model that is truly alone at the frontier in capabilities — something like Mythos when it was announced — also be open-weight poses serious risks as we go into the unknown of capabilities. Models are going to progress very fast and it is increasingly hard to measure their total capabilities. We are then stuck in a world where we are trying to thread the needle on open models. It’s reasonable to not want something so powerful to be diffused globally in an instant, but meanwhile the makers of the models are incentivized to hype their capabilities, and their competitors are incentivized to hype their risks. It all comes down to careful measurement and proactive hardening of society to risk vectors.This careening train of policy debates, model releases, and raucous reactions is only going to continue from today. We’ve been on a train of rapid progress, where all the key ideas of how AI should play out are tested, since the release of Claude Opus 4.5 last December, which sent us down the agentic pathway. The key to making good decisions here is evaluation capabilities, independent of the companies with the largest financial stakes. One of many actions needed then, as we enter the AGI era of AI governance, is an Operation Warp Speed style approach of bootstrapping state capacity (and other independent actors) that can evaluate models accurately, and study emerging risks.Open-weight models, by accelerating the diffusion of capabilities, are a massive escalation in the good and the potential bad of AI. For now, the bad side of frontier language models has been largely hypothetical, but that will not always remain the case. Having open weight models be slightly behind the closed frontier is our natural buffer to mitigate the risks. The key point is that we must collectively act to mitigate potential harms as they appear, and whether open-weight models are 3 or 6 or 9 months behind, that is still a very short timeline. If we regulate open-weight models heavy-handedly, I suspect much of the world will be lulled into thinking we no longer need to act. All we would’ve done is slightly delayed the inevitable — open models will continue to cross all the key capability thresholds eventually and regardless of legality.Understanding and benefiting from this open-closed dance must be a collective action from the AI community across all sectors of power and influence over the coming years.Kimi K3 is a watershed moment because frontier open-weight models are now real. Many hypotheses will be tested on where risks of open-weight models truly land — I suspect it’ll be narrower than many expect, and many risks of AI will still be proliferated by closed and “safer” APIs. The evaluation of these risks will evolve in time with an acceleration of AI’s integration in our economy. We cannot get one without the other, and we will continue to get both.With this, 2025 was when open models started to be taken more seriously — especially when China leaped ahead with such a clear lead — as people realized that it would not be a unipolar world, with only American, closed AI labs determining the trajectory. 2026 is when those previously discussed, potential risks and accelerations due to truly frontier, open-weight models landed. 1A large portion of the response was for the fourth bullet, which compared the inevitable outcome of open models to AI communism, which I think missed the mark. Specifically, the use of the word communism without explanation caused much of the blowback.

blocks.html_en: On Thursday July 16th, Moonshot AI released their latest flagship model Kimi K3. K3 is a 2.8T parameter MoE model which will have its weights released on July 27th. Much of this article follows as a reflection on the state of the ecosystem, under the assumption that Moonshot keeps their promise of the weights release date. This is a more extreme view of the equilibrium, and many of the results end up in a middle ground if the state of affairs is that China has similarly powerful, but closed models (i.e. K3 is never released).

sentences.en: On Thursday July 16th, Moonshot AI released their latest flagship model Kimi K3.
sentences.zh: 7 月 16 日星期四，Moonshot AI 发布了最新旗舰机型 Kimi K3。

sentences.en: K3 is a 2.8T parameter MoE model which will have its weights released on July 27th.
sentences.zh: K3是一款2.8T参数MoE车型，重量将于7月27日发布。

sentences.en: Much of this article follows as a reflection on the state of the ecosystem, under the assumption that Moonshot keeps their promise of the weights release date.
sentences.zh: 本文的大部分内容是对生态系统状态的反思，假设 Moonshot 信守了权重发布日期的承诺。

sentences.en: This is a more extreme view of the equilibrium, and many of the results end up in a middle ground if the state of affairs is that China has similarly powerful, but closed models (i.e. K3 is never released).
sentences.zh: 这是一种更为极端的均衡观点，如果中国拥有同样强大但封闭的模型（即 K3 从未发布），则许多结果最终会处于中间立场。

blocks.html_en: The key fact is that either the open-to-closed or American-to-Chinese model performance gap has been reduced from the debated 6-9 months to something shorter, say 3-5 months.

sentences.en: The key fact is that either the open-to-closed or American-to-Chinese model performance gap has been reduced from the debated 6-9 months to something shorter, say 3-5 months.
sentences.zh: 关键事实是，开放与封闭或美国与中国模型的性能差距已经从有争议的 6-9 个月缩短到更短的时间，比如 3-5 个月。

blocks.html_en: From the release materials, it is clear that K3 is a true frontier model. It will be the closest open models have been to the frontier since DeepSeek R1. DeepSeek R1 was a different story. This was a Chinese lab being extremely quick to pivot to reasoning models and release one faster than many American companies. Kimi K3 an example of a Chinese lab executing on scaling the known areas: data, algorithms, architecture, tools, environments, etc.

sentences.en: From the release materials, it is clear that K3 is a true frontier model.
sentences.zh: 从发布的资料来看，K3是一款真正的前沿车型。

sentences.en: It will be the closest open models have been to the frontier since DeepSeek R1.
sentences.zh: 这将是自 DeepSeek R1 以来最接近前沿的开放模型。

sentences.en: DeepSeek R1 was a different story.
sentences.zh: DeepSeek R1 则是另一回事。

sentences.en: This was a Chinese lab being extremely quick to pivot to reasoning models and release one faster than many American companies.
sentences.zh: 这是一家中国实验室，其转向推理模型的速度非常快，并且比许多美国公司更快地发布了模型。

sentences.en: Kimi K3 an example of a Chinese lab executing on scaling the known areas: data, algorithms, architecture, tools, environments, etc.
sentences.zh: Kimi K3 是中国实验室执行扩展已知领域的示例：数据、算法、架构、工具、环境等。

blocks.html_en: Kimi K3 comes in at #2 overall on the Vals AI index, #3 overall on Artificial Analysis’s Intelligence Index (only beaten by Claude Fable and GPT-5.6 Sol Max while being cheaper), #1 overall in Frontend Code Arena, and more impressive results. Moonshot AI is going toe to toe with Anthropic and OpenAI with far, far fewer resources.

sentences.en: Kimi K3 comes in at #2 overall on the Vals AI index, #3 overall on Artificial Analysis’s Intelligence Index (only beaten by Claude Fable and GPT-5.6 Sol Max while being cheaper), #1 overall in Frontend Code Arena, and more impressive results.
sentences.zh: Kimi K3 在 Vals AI 指数中总体排名第 2，在人工分析智能指数中总体排名第 3（仅落后于 Claude Fable 和 GPT-5.6 Sol Max，但价格更便宜），在前端代码竞技场中总体排名第 1，并且取得了更令人印象深刻的结果。

sentences.en: Moonshot AI is going toe to toe with Anthropic and OpenAI with far, far fewer resources.
sentences.zh: Moonshot AI 正在以少得多的资源与 Anthropic 和 OpenAI 正面交锋。

blocks.html_en: It is clearly the strongest open model ever released. It should be clear looking at this model that if adversarial distillation from the closed frontier models in the U.S. contributed, it is at most to a relatively small degree. AI observers who followed the distillation panic and came away with the wrong conclusion that Chinese AI labs are only producing good models due to IP theft are in for an awakening – that Chinese companies are extremely good at building models in the same way the leading American companies are. Moonshot AI is solving many of the same problems that folks at OpenAI or Anthropic are solving. I’m confident there will be more distillation discussion, and pressure, but the evidence is now out that Chinese companies can do more than just fast following.

sentences.en: It is clearly the strongest open model ever released.
sentences.zh: 这显然是迄今为止发布的最强开放模型。

sentences.en: It should be clear looking at this model that if adversarial distillation from the closed frontier models in the U.S. contributed, it is at most to a relatively small degree.
sentences.zh: 从这个模型来看，应该清楚的是，如果美国封闭边界模型的对抗性蒸馏有所贡献，那么最多也只是相对较小的程度。

sentences.en: AI observers who followed the distillation panic and came away with the wrong conclusion that Chinese AI labs are only producing good models due to IP theft are in for an awakening – that Chinese companies are extremely good at building models in the same way the leading American companies are.
sentences.zh: 那些追随蒸馏恐慌并得出错误结论的人工智能观察家，认为中国人工智能实验室只是因为知识产权盗窃而生产出好的模型，现在他们醒悟了——中国公司非常擅长像美国领先公司一样构建模型。

sentences.en: Moonshot AI is solving many of the same problems that folks at OpenAI or Anthropic are solving.
sentences.zh: Moonshot AI 正在解决 OpenAI 或 Anthropic 人员正在解决的许多相同问题。

sentences.en: I’m confident there will be more distillation discussion, and pressure, but the evidence is now out that Chinese companies can do more than just fast following.
sentences.zh: 我相信将会有更多的蒸馏讨论和压力，但现在有证据表明，中国公司可以做的不仅仅是快速跟随。

blocks.html_en: Meeting some of the core Kimi team on my trip to China, it was clear to me that they had incredible culture, some would say aura, and a freedom to express it – within the constraints of a GPU-limited environment. Where building models is so much of a scaling game, much of the ability to build a good model still comes down individual execution, motivation, and expression. Having visited them, this result is less surprising. Having visited many AI companies, very few have a culture that you can immediately pick up like this.

sentences.en: Meeting some of the core Kimi team on my trip to China, it was clear to me that they had incredible culture, some would say aura, and a freedom to express it – within the constraints of a GPU-limited environment.
sentences.zh: 在我的中国之行中见到了一些 Kimi 核心团队的成员，我很清楚他们拥有令人难以置信的文化，有些人会说是光环，并且在 GPU 有限的环境的限制下可以自由地表达它。

sentences.en: Where building models is so much of a scaling game, much of the ability to build a good model still comes down individual execution, motivation, and expression.
sentences.zh: 构建模型在很大程度上是一种扩展游戏，构建良好模型的能力在很大程度上仍然取决于个人的执行、动机和表达。

sentences.en: Having visited them, this result is less surprising.
sentences.zh: 拜访过他们后，这个结果就不那么令人惊讶了。

sentences.en: Having visited many AI companies, very few have a culture that you can immediately pick up like this.
sentences.zh: 走访过很多AI公司，很少有公司有这样的文化可以让你立刻领会。

blocks.html_en: At the same time, China’s AI adoption trends started later than those in the U.S. So, while all the Chinese labs have way less compute than their counterparts in the U.S., more of it can certainly go to training. When I joked around about how much compute an average researcher at OpenAI could have – say a few thousand H100 equivalent machines – the researchers at Kimi were shocked. The org chart and approach to building the Kimi models surely reflect this, but it is difficult to tease out what this looks like without substantial proprietary information.

sentences.en: At the same time, China’s AI adoption trends started later than those in the U.S. So, while all the Chinese labs have way less compute than their counterparts in the U.S., more of it can certainly go to training.
sentences.zh: 与此同时，中国的人工智能采用趋势比美国起步晚。因此，虽然所有中国实验室的计算能力都比美国同行少得多，但肯定有更多的计算能力可以用于培训。

sentences.en: When I joked around about how much compute an average researcher at OpenAI could have – say a few thousand H100 equivalent machines – the researchers at Kimi were shocked.
sentences.zh: 当我开玩笑说 OpenAI 的研究人员平均可以拥有多少计算能力（比如几千台 H100 等效机器）时，Kimi 的研究人员感到震惊。

sentences.en: The org chart and approach to building the Kimi models surely reflect this, but it is difficult to tease out what this looks like without substantial proprietary information.
sentences.zh: 构建 Kimi 模型的组织结构图和方法肯定反映了这一点，但如果没有大量专有信息，很难弄清楚它是什么样子。

blocks.html_en: Share

Many people started following China’s AI scene relatively recently, so they can reach the conclusion that releasing models openly is their core strategy. In fact, I think most labs have a core strategy far closer to Anthropic or OpenAI – build the best intelligence possible. Having followed and engaged with the Chinese labs for years now, the best explanation for their original turn to releasing their models openly is practicality. They needed to release the models openly to get adoption, attention, and feedback (especially in the high-value, Bay Area market).

sentences.en: Many people started following China’s AI scene relatively recently, so they can reach the conclusion that releasing models openly is their core strategy.
sentences.zh: 很多人最近才开始关注中国的人工智能场景，因此他们可以得出这样的结论：公开发布模型是他们的核心策略。

sentences.en: In fact, I think most labs have a core strategy far closer to Anthropic or OpenAI – build the best intelligence possible.
sentences.zh: 事实上，我认为大多数实验室都有一个更接近 Anthropic 或 OpenAI 的核心策略——构建尽可能最好的智能。

sentences.en: Having followed and engaged with the Chinese labs for years now, the best explanation for their original turn to releasing their models openly is practicality.
sentences.zh: 多年来一直关注并与中国实验室合作，他们最初公开发布模型的最佳解释是实用性。

sentences.en: They needed to release the models openly to get adoption, attention, and feedback (especially in the high-value, Bay Area market).
sentences.zh: 他们需要公开发布模型以获得采用、关注和反馈（特别是在高价值的湾区市场）。

blocks.html_en: For a long time, there had been very limited policy in China explaining the role of open-source AI, and what could be the “country-level strategy.” To my knowledge, no senior leaders had commented on open-source AI publicly. This changed this week too, as Xi Jinping gave a keynote address at the World AI Conference (WAIC), and very directly committed the future of China’s AI ecosystem to open-source and global diffusion. This commitment to the status quo, the same week as the announcement of the strongest open-weight model to date, is a clear mark in the early history of modern AI.

sentences.en: For a long time, there had been very limited policy in China explaining the role of open-source AI, and what could be the “country-level strategy.”
sentences.zh: 长期以来，中国解释开源人工智能作用以及“国家级战略”的政策非常有限。

sentences.en: To my knowledge, no senior leaders had commented on open-source AI publicly.
sentences.zh: 据我所知，没有任何高层领导公开评论过开源人工智能。

sentences.en: This changed this week too, as Xi Jinping gave a keynote address at the World AI Conference (WAIC), and very directly committed the future of China’s AI ecosystem to open-source and global diffusion.
sentences.zh: 本周，随着习近平在世界人工智能大会（WAIC）上发表主旨演讲，这种情况也发生了变化，并非常直接地承诺中国人工智能生态系统的未来将致力于开源和全球传播。

sentences.en: This commitment to the status quo, the same week as the announcement of the strongest open-weight model to date, is a clear mark in the early history of modern AI.
sentences.zh: 这种对现状的承诺，在宣布迄今为止最强的开放权重模型的同一周，是现代人工智能早期历史上的一个明显标志。

blocks.html_en: This comes during a time period where many potential paths forward have been discussed for the Chinese AI industry – Will they stay open? Can they keep up with the American labs in scaling? Is there a growing revenue market in China? With these, the focus has been on China’s risk tolerance, the companies’ ability to monetize, and any closely related reason for a company to stop releasing their best models openly.

sentences.en: This comes during a time period where many potential paths forward have been discussed for the Chinese AI industry – Will they stay open?
sentences.zh: 在此期间，中国人工智能行业讨论了许多潜在的前进道路——它们会保持开放吗？

sentences.en: Can they keep up with the American labs in scaling?
sentences.zh: 他们在规模化方面能跟上美国实验室的步伐吗？

sentences.en: Is there a growing revenue market in China?
sentences.zh: 中国的收入市场是否正在增长？

sentences.en: With these, the focus has been on China’s risk tolerance, the companies’ ability to monetize, and any closely related reason for a company to stop releasing their best models openly.
sentences.zh: 其中，重点是中国的风险承受能力、公司的货币化能力，以及公司停止公开发布最佳模型的任何密切相关的原因。

blocks.html_en: In tying Xi’s commitment in time to a very strong model, China has implicitly commented on its risk tolerance with respect to releasing open-weight models. For the time being, it is a read into the perceived risks of topics like strong cybersecurity capabilities (or bio-dangers) within the Chinese system.

sentences.en: In tying Xi’s commitment in time to a very strong model, China has implicitly commented on its risk tolerance with respect to releasing open-weight models.
sentences.zh: 在将习近平的承诺及时与一个非常强大的模型联系起来的过程中，中国含蓄地评论了其在发布开放权重模型方面的风险承受能力。

sentences.en: For the time being, it is a read into the perceived risks of topics like strong cybersecurity capabilities (or bio-dangers) within the Chinese system.
sentences.zh: 目前，它是对中国体系内强大的网络安全能力（或生物危险）等主题的感知风险的解读。

blocks.html_en: The simplest explanation is that China’s government is definitely following potential risks from the models closely – likely with more technical scope than the US government’s vibe regulation – and would take action if it measured risk. The simple explanation is that they do not find current frontier models to have meaningful risk.

sentences.en: The simplest explanation is that China’s government is definitely following potential risks from the models closely – likely with more technical scope than the US government’s vibe regulation – and would take action if it measured risk.
sentences.zh: 最简单的解释是，中国政府肯定正在密切关注模型中的潜在风险（可能比美国政府的氛围监管具有更多的技术范围），并且如果衡量风险就会采取行动。

sentences.en: The simple explanation is that they do not find current frontier models to have meaningful risk.
sentences.zh: 简单的解释是，他们认为当前的前沿模型不存在有意义的风险。

blocks.html_en: At the same time, China’s economic decision makers think having AI adoption is good, so they can make profits on the industry later – after growing distribution (as China has done for cars, solar, advanced manufacturing, and many areas in recent history).

blocks.html_en: Many of the narrators guiding the discussion on AI have clear incentives to depress the perceived capabilities of the best, open AI models. Dean Ball – who is personally supportive of open models, but now works at OpenAI – had a widely commented on post with some reflections on Kimi, where he said the following on open models. It is important to understand the statement, as it focuses the role of open models in the economic side of the AI buildout. Dean says:1

sentences.en: It is important to understand the statement, as it focuses the role of open models in the economic side of the AI buildout.
sentences.zh: 理解这一声明很重要，因为它重点关注开放模型在人工智能建设的经济方面的作用。

sentences.en: Dean says:1

blocks.html_en: 1. Open-weight models are inherently decelerationist, and I’m continually surprised to see the so-called “accelerationists” so excited about open-weight models.

blocks.html_en: Explaining why open-weight models are a form of decelerationism is important to understanding the coming world order. He is right.

sentences.zh: 解释为什么开放权重模型是减速主义的一种形式对于理解即将到来的世界秩序很重要。

sentences.en: He is right.
sentences.zh: 他是对的。

blocks.html_en: Open models are decelerationist economically for the frontier labs, which will slow the net investment and capex rollout for AI. This is due to the fact that strong open-weight AI models massively reduce the margin potential for the closed labs. This has two effects. First, the AI labs have fewer profits to re-invest into future models. Second, the market sees the terminal value of these companies as being lower, so they will kneecap future fundraising rounds. These together will slow timelines to the most transformative AI models, but I do not see them as strong enough effects to stop OpenAI and Anthropic from being a few of the top valued companies in the world.

sentences.en: Open models are decelerationist economically for the frontier labs, which will slow the net investment and capex rollout for AI.
sentences.zh: 对于前沿实验室来说，开放模型在经济上具有减速作用，这将减缓人工智能的净投资和资本支出的推出。

sentences.en: This is due to the fact that strong open-weight AI models massively reduce the margin potential for the closed labs.
sentences.zh: 这是因为强大的开放权重人工智能模型大大降低了封闭实验室的利润潜力。

sentences.en: This has two effects.
sentences.zh: 这有两个效果。

sentences.en: First, the AI labs have fewer profits to re-invest into future models.
sentences.zh: 首先，人工智能实验室用于再投资未来模型的利润较少。

sentences.en: Second, the market sees the terminal value of these companies as being lower, so they will kneecap future fundraising rounds.
sentences.zh: 其次，市场认为这些公司的最终价值较低，因此他们将限制未来的融资轮次。

sentences.en: These together will slow timelines to the most transformative AI models, but I do not see them as strong enough effects to stop OpenAI and Anthropic from being a few of the top valued companies in the world.
sentences.zh: 这些因素共同将减缓最具变革性的人工智能模型的进程，但我认为它们的影响不足以阻止 OpenAI 和 Anthropic 成为世界上最有价值的公司之一。

blocks.html_en: These, to me, are a net good for society. As open-weight models are accelerationist for AI diffusion across the economy by having the entry price for intelligence at a certain level of performance be lower. Open models also encourage customization. The thing is that this type of diffusion is by its nature far slower than the frontier AI labs products, who sell tools used directly by developers. The potential for open models is for nearly every business to use them to craft domain-specific agents. This economic diffusion takes an extremely long time! I’ve described this as open-weight models being on a much slower starting, but potentially bigger exponential. The problem is, if closed models get too far ahead in raw capabilities, this ability to customize can be moot.

sentences.en: These, to me, are a net good for society.
sentences.zh: 对我来说，这些都是对社会的净好处。

sentences.en: As open-weight models are accelerationist for AI diffusion across the economy by having the entry price for intelligence at a certain level of performance be lower.
sentences.zh: 由于开放权重模型通过降低特定性能水平的智能入门价格，加速了人工智能在整个经济中的扩散。

sentences.en: Open models also encourage customization.
sentences.zh: 开放模型也鼓励定制。

sentences.en: The thing is that this type of diffusion is by its nature far slower than the frontier AI labs products, who sell tools used directly by developers.

sentences.en: The potential for open models is for nearly every business to use them to craft domain-specific agents.
sentences.zh: 开放模型的潜力在于，几乎每个企业都可以使用它们来构建特定领域的代理。

sentences.en: This economic diffusion takes an extremely long time!
sentences.zh: 这种经济扩散需要非常长的时间！

sentences.en: I’ve described this as open-weight models being on a much slower starting, but potentially bigger exponential.
sentences.zh: 我将其描述为开放权重模型，其启动速度要慢得多，但指数可能更大。

sentences.en: The problem is, if closed models get too far ahead in raw capabilities, this ability to customize can be moot.
sentences.zh: 问题是，如果封闭模型在原始功能方面领先太多，那么这种定制能力就没有实际意义。

blocks.html_en: The combination of increased diffusion and decreased concentration of power in the AI labs I see to be very positive for the AI transition. It gives us more time to figure out the hard problems of new capabilities and lets more stakeholders impact the story – any one company is very likely to have issues with controlling the world’s most important technology safely.

sentences.en: The combination of increased diffusion and decreased concentration of power in the AI labs I see to be very positive for the AI transition.
sentences.zh: 我认为人工智能实验室中权力扩散的增加​​和权力集中度的降低对人工智能的转型非常积极。

sentences.en: It gives us more time to figure out the hard problems of new capabilities and lets more stakeholders impact the story – any one company is very likely to have issues with controlling the world’s most important technology safely.
sentences.zh: 它让我们有更多的时间来解决新功能的难题，并让更多的利益相关者影响故事——任何一家公司都很可能在安全控制世界上最重要的技术方面遇到问题。

blocks.html_en: It is, of course, important to me in this world for the best models to still be made by the U.S. companies, which will allow the US to control the trajectory of the technology and its values. I also expect this to be the case, as the U.S. has larger capital markets that are willing to invest in AI (and a growing share of profits), but it is not a given.

sentences.en: It is, of course, important to me in this world for the best models to still be made by the U.S. companies, which will allow the US to control the trajectory of the technology and its values.
sentences.zh: 当然，对我来说，在这个世界上，美国公司仍然制造最好的模型很重要，这将使美国能够控制技术及其价值的轨迹。

sentences.en: I also expect this to be the case, as the U.S. has larger capital markets that are willing to invest in AI (and a growing share of profits), but it is not a given.
sentences.zh: 我也预计情况会如此，因为美国拥有更大的资本市场，愿意投资人工智能（以及越来越多的利润份额），但这并不是必然的。

blocks.html_en: Kimi K3 is built on Kimi Delta Attention (KDA) and Attention Residuals (AttnRes), two architectural updates designed to improve how information flows across sequence length and model depth. We have also scaled up Mixture of Experts (MoE) sparsity, effectively activating 16 out of 896 experts when paired with a Stable LatentMoE framework. Together with refined training and data recipes, these structural changes yield an approximate 2.5× improvement in overall scaling efficiency compared to Kimi K2, allowing the model to convert compute into intelligence more effectively.

sentences.zh: Kimi K3 基于 Kimi Delta Attention (KDA) 和 Attention Residuals (AttnRes) 构建，这两个架构更新旨在改善信息在序列长度和模型深度上的流动方式。

blocks.html_en: Training efficiency really adds up. They will result in continued, incredible steps for the models.

blocks.html_en: As an aside, tracing the path of this particular innovation through the ecosystem is an interesting example. Kimi Delta Attention (KDA) was introduced in the Kimi Linear paper, which is similar to the Gated DeltaNet used for Olmo Hybrid (my last Olmo model while at Ai2). Qwen’s latest models switched to a related architecture and the recent Nemotron models also are hybrid (but still closer to Mamba than Gated DeltaNet). It’s awesome to see new architecture ideas like these, which were heavily progressed by academia, get so quickly translated into frontier-scale models. Gated Delta Networks were introduced in late 2024, building on ideas from Mamba. By mid 2026, they’re in frontier models.

sentences.en: As an aside, tracing the path of this particular innovation through the ecosystem is an interesting example.
sentences.zh: 顺便说一句，通过生态系统追踪这一特定创新的路径是一个有趣的例子。

sentences.en: Kimi Delta Attention (KDA) was introduced in the Kimi Linear paper, which is similar to the Gated DeltaNet used for Olmo Hybrid (my last Olmo model while at Ai2).
sentences.zh: Kimi Delta Attention (KDA) 在 Kimi Linear 论文中被引入，它类似于 Olmo Hybrid（我在 Ai2 时的最后一个 Olmo 模型）中使用的 Gated DeltaNet。

sentences.zh: 很高兴看到像这样的新架构思想在学术界取得了巨大进展，并如此迅速地转化为前沿规模的模型。

sentences.en: Gated Delta Networks were introduced in late 2024, building on ideas from Mamba.
sentences.zh: 基于 Mamba 的想法，门控 Delta 网络于 2024 年末推出。

sentences.en: By mid 2026, they’re in frontier models.
sentences.zh: 到 2026 年中期，它们将成为前沿型号。

blocks.html_en: I chose to focus on this example, partially because the Kimi team put a cool number to innovations between models, but primarily to give space to a broader discussion of China’s resource efficiency.

sentences.en: I chose to focus on this example, partially because the Kimi team put a cool number to innovations between models, but primarily to give space to a broader discussion of China’s resource efficiency.
sentences.zh: 我选择重点关注这个例子，部分原因是 Kimi 团队对模型之间的创新给出了一个很酷的数字，但主要是为了为更广泛地讨论中国的资源效率提供空间。

blocks.html_en: It is becoming clear that the Chinese labs are far more capital efficient. In a world where scaling laws dictate that intelligence is proportional to effective capital – which buys compute, data, & talent – that may be the greatest strength your AI industry could ever have. There are many possible explanations for why this is the case, such as Chinese researchers being paid less while being more effective at LLM research puzzles, but we will probably never get such specific reasons.

sentences.en: It is becoming clear that the Chinese labs are far more capital efficient.
sentences.zh: 越来越明显的是，中国实验室的资本效率要高得多。

sentences.en: In a world where scaling laws dictate that intelligence is proportional to effective capital – which buys compute, data, & talent – that may be the greatest strength your AI industry could ever have.
sentences.zh: 在一个规模法则规定智力与有效资本（购买计算、数据和人才）成正比的世界中，这可能是人工智能行业所能拥有的最大优势。

sentences.en: There are many possible explanations for why this is the case, such as Chinese researchers being paid less while being more effective at LLM research puzzles, but we will probably never get such specific reasons.
sentences.zh: 对于为什么会出现这种情况，有很多可能的解释，比如中国研究人员的报酬较低，但在法学硕士研究难题上更有效，但我们可能永远不会得到这样的具体原因。

blocks.html_en: Since writing my notes on China, I’m hearing more about an emerging data industry in China (far behind the billion dollar budgets of Anthropic for data) and that Chinese labs have access to meaningful training compute (by skirting export controls). Chinese companies do not have the same inference demand (until recently, as Moonshot AI had to pause new subscriptions for access to their K3 model - while the API is still live), so much more of their compute could go to training. These areas impinge heavily on the truth of the ability of the labs, but we have very limited measurement into them.

sentences.en: Since writing my notes on China, I’m hearing more about an emerging data industry in China (far behind the billion dollar budgets of Anthropic for data) and that Chinese labs have access to meaningful training compute (by skirting export controls).
sentences.zh: 自从撰写关于中国的笔记以来，我听到更多关于中国新兴数据产业的信息（远远落后于 Anthropic 数十亿美元的数据预算），并且中国实验室可以获得有意义的训练计算（通过规避出口管制）。

sentences.en: Chinese companies do not have the same inference demand (until recently, as Moonshot AI had to pause new subscriptions for access to their K3 model - while the API is still live), so much more of their compute could go to training.
sentences.zh: 中国公司没有同样的推理需求（直到最近，Moonshot AI 不得不暂停新的订阅以访问其 K3 模型 - 而 API 仍然有效），因此他们的更多计算可以用于培训。

sentences.en: These areas impinge heavily on the truth of the ability of the labs, but we have very limited measurement into them.
sentences.zh: 这些领域严重影响了实验室能力的真实性，但我们对它们的衡量非常有限。

blocks.html_en: The facts on the ground are that these Chinese labs have raised orders of magnitude less capital than any slice of the American AI ecosystem. The most direct comparisons are to OpenAI and Anthropic, who have slightly better public models. Others, such as Google and Meta have the largest cash flows in the history of business, and are behind on building models. As for American neolabs, the picture is even more competitive – Thinking Machines released their first model recently, Inkling, which is strong but not in the same class as Kimi K3.

sentences.en: The facts on the ground are that these Chinese labs have raised orders of magnitude less capital than any slice of the American AI ecosystem.
sentences.zh: 事实是，这些中国实验室筹集的资金比美国人工智能生态系统的任何部分都要少几个数量级。

sentences.en: The most direct comparisons are to OpenAI and Anthropic, who have slightly better public models.
sentences.zh: 最直接的比较是 OpenAI 和 Anthropic，它们的公共模型稍好一些。

sentences.en: Others, such as Google and Meta have the largest cash flows in the history of business, and are behind on building models.
sentences.zh: 其他公司，例如谷歌和 Meta，拥有商业史上最大的现金流，但在构建模型方面却落后了。

sentences.en: As for American neolabs, the picture is even more competitive – Thinking Machines released their first model recently, Inkling, which is strong but not in the same class as Kimi K3.
sentences.zh: 至于美国neolabs，竞争更加激烈——Thinking Machines最近发布了他们的第一款机型Inkling，虽然实力强劲，但与Kimi K3不在同一级别。

blocks.html_en: These American companies with more resources could still catch up, but you need to strongly weigh the public measurements we have of model quality and not resort to hope – which often reflects a bias. If the Chinese labs do have a latent advantage, they could continue to utilize that to build even stronger models than all the competitors! Many outcomes are plausible and K3 should increase most people’s probability that China can outright lead in AI capabilities in the near future on the back of more efficient training efforts – even if it’s not your most likely predicted outcome.

sentences.en: These American companies with more resources could still catch up, but you need to strongly weigh the public measurements we have of model quality and not resort to hope – which often reflects a bias.
sentences.zh: 这些拥有更多资源的美国公司仍然可以迎头赶上，但你需要认真权衡我们对模型质量的公开衡量标准，而不是寄希望于——这通常反映出一种偏见。

sentences.en: If the Chinese labs do have a latent advantage, they could continue to utilize that to build even stronger models than all the competitors!
sentences.zh: 如果中国实验室确实拥有潜在优势，他们可以继续利用这一优势来构建比所有竞争对手更强大的模型！

sentences.en: Many outcomes are plausible and K3 should increase most people’s probability that China can outright lead in AI capabilities in the near future on the back of more efficient training efforts – even if it’s not your most likely predicted outcome.
sentences.zh: 许多结果都是合理的，K3 应该会增加大多数人的可能性，即在更有效的培训工作的支持下，中国在不久的将来能够在人工智能能力方面彻底领先——即使这不是你最有可能预测的结果。

blocks.html_en: A big contributor to the capital efficiency is likely in the approach, where American labs are spending meaningful energy in pushing the frontier in dramatic, big steps, and the Chinese labs are more focused on catching up — this catch-up is cheaper. Just as the student model can outperform the teacher in distillation generally (not limited to the adversarial distillation of the Chinese labs), an approach of “trying to catch up” rather than “invent the next paradigm” could lead to stronger models.

sentences.en: A big contributor to the capital efficiency is likely in the approach, where American labs are spending meaningful energy in pushing the frontier in dramatic, big steps, and the Chinese labs are more focused on catching up — this catch-up is cheaper.
sentences.zh: 资本效率的一个重要贡献者可能是这种方法，美国实验室正在花费大量精力以戏剧性的、大步推进前沿，而中国实验室更专注于追赶——这种追赶成本更低。

sentences.en: Just as the student model can outperform the teacher in distillation generally (not limited to the adversarial distillation of the Chinese labs), an approach of “trying to catch up” rather than “invent the next paradigm” could lead to stronger models.
sentences.zh: 正如学生模型在蒸馏方面通常可以超越老师一样（不仅限于中国实验室的对抗性蒸馏），“试图赶上”而不是“发明下一个范式”的方法可能会产生更强大的模型。

blocks.html_en: The weekend after the Kimi K3 release, while writing this and discussing the events broadly, Alibaba announced that a 2.4 trillion parameter Qwen 3.8 model is coming soon with open-weights. Historically, Alibaba has kept their largest models as API-only offerings via their cloud business, so this is another big vibe shift opening the doors to the next chapter of the open model economy. Even if the model is behind Kimi K3 on benchmarks, it signifies that Chinese companies may not only be maintaining the status quo for their open model strategy, but leaning further into it.

sentences.en: The weekend after the Kimi K3 release, while writing this and discussing the events broadly, Alibaba announced that a 2.4 trillion parameter Qwen 3.8 model is coming soon with open-weights.
sentences.zh: Kimi K3 发布后的周末，在撰写本文并广泛讨论这些事件时，阿里巴巴宣布即将推出 2.4 万亿参数的 Qwen 3.8 模型，并且具有开放权重。

sentences.en: Historically, Alibaba has kept their largest models as API-only offerings via their cloud business, so this is another big vibe shift opening the doors to the next chapter of the open model economy.
sentences.zh: 从历史上看，阿里巴巴一直通过其云业务将其最大的模型保留为仅提供 API 的产品，因此这是另一个重大的氛围转变，为开放模型经济的下一章打开了大门。

sentences.en: Even if the model is behind Kimi K3 on benchmarks, it signifies that Chinese companies may not only be maintaining the status quo for their open model strategy, but leaning further into it.
sentences.zh: 即使该车型在基准测试中落后于Kimi K3，也意味着中国企业可能不仅会维持开放车型战略的现状，而且会进一步向开放模式倾斜。

blocks.html_en: If this Qwen 3.8 model releases soon, i.e. before the next Gemini model, it could push Google to the 8th position on the leaderboard of labs with the smartest models – a list that China has been climbing. There are other rumors of more strong Chinese models soon, with DeepSeek V4 expected to graduate out of it’s “preview” version.

sentences.en: If this Qwen 3.8 model releases soon, i.e. before the next Gemini model, it could push Google to the 8th position on the leaderboard of labs with the smartest models – a list that China has been climbing.
sentences.zh: 如果这个 Qwen 3.8 模型很快发布，即在下一个 Gemini 模型之前发布，它可能会将谷歌推到最智能模型实验室排行榜上的第八位——中国一直在攀升这一名单。

sentences.en: There are other rumors of more strong Chinese models soon, with DeepSeek V4 expected to graduate out of it’s “preview” version.
sentences.zh: 还有其他传言称，很快会有更强大的中国模型，DeepSeek V4 预计将从其“预览”版本中毕业。

blocks.html_en: I think that if Claude Mythos was released as an open-weight model today, the negative outcomes would be relatively minor. This is a somewhat challenging opinion to hold, as we have very limited public cybersecurity evaluations and it is a complicated ecosystem (and because I trust many people at Anthropic). I still stand by it. The risks have been over-hyped.

sentences.en: I think that if Claude Mythos was released as an open-weight model today, the negative outcomes would be relatively minor.
sentences.zh: 我认为如果 Claude Mythos 今天作为开放权重模型发布，负面结果将相对较小。

sentences.en: This is a somewhat challenging opinion to hold, as we have very limited public cybersecurity evaluations and it is a complicated ecosystem (and because I trust many people at Anthropic).
sentences.zh: 这是一个有点具有挑战性的观点，因为我们的公共网络安全评估非常有限，而且这是一个复杂的生态系统（而且因为我信任 Anthropic 的很多人）。

sentences.en: I still stand by it.
sentences.zh: 我仍然坚持这一点。

sentences.en: The risks have been over-hyped.
sentences.zh: 风险被夸大了。

blocks.html_en: The problem is that this will not always be the case for the strongest AI models. Far stronger models are coming — and with them increased risks — so it is an incredibly safe equilibrium for the best models to be accessed in a controlled, closed manner several months ahead of similar open-weight models.

sentences.en: The problem is that this will not always be the case for the strongest AI models.
sentences.zh: 问题是，对于最强大的人工智能模型来说，情况并不总是如此。

sentences.en: Far stronger models are coming — and with them increased risks — so it is an incredibly safe equilibrium for the best models to be accessed in a controlled, closed manner several months ahead of similar open-weight models.
sentences.zh: 更强大的模型即将到来——随之而来的风险也随之增加——因此，对于最好的模型来说，比类似的开放权重模型提前几个月以受控、封闭的方式访问是一种令人难以置信的安全平衡。

blocks.html_en: Open-weight models which are very controllable by the user will always be coming — you cannot effectively ban digital products, especially from bad actors — as AI training has proven globally accessible longer than many analysts expected.

sentences.en: Open-weight models which are very controllable by the user will always be coming — you cannot effectively ban digital products, especially from bad actors — as AI training has proven globally accessible longer than many analysts expected.
sentences.zh: 用户非常可控的开放权重模型总是会出现——你无法有效地禁止数字产品，尤其是来自不良行为者的——因为事实证明，人工智能培训在全球范围内的普及时间比许多分析师预期的要长。

blocks.html_en: Still, as I write this, the government continues to flirt with more measures aimed at restricting open-weight models in the U.S. The latest is from Axios:

sentences.en: Still, as I write this, the government continues to flirt with more measures aimed at restricting open-weight models in the U.S. The latest is from Axios:
sentences.zh: 尽管如此，在我撰写本文时，政府仍在继续考虑采取更多措施，旨在限制美国的开放重量模型。最新消息来自 Axios：

blocks.html_en:
Behind the scenes: The Commerce Department last year considered adding multiple Chinese AI labs to its “Entity List,” which would effectively cut off U.S. access without a license, a source close to the administration told Axios.

  • The National Security Agency and White House Office of the National Cyber Director also considered putting out an advisory on Chinese AI lab threats last year, practically discouraging U.S. companies from using their tech, the source said.

  • The White House considered implementing an executive order saying U.S. companies could only host Chinese models if they could guarantee security and take liability if it were breached, the source added.

  • Commerce last summer also circulated draft rules within the administration leveraging its authorities to secure domestic supply chains to target Chinese open-source models, another source close to the administration said.

sentences.en: Behind the scenes: The Commerce Department last year considered adding multiple Chinese AI labs to its “Entity List,” which would effectively cut off U.S. access without a license, a source close to the administration told Axios.The National Security Agency and White House Office of the National Cyber Director also considered putting out an advisory on Chinese AI lab threats last year, practically discouraging U.S. companies from using their tech, the source said.The White House considered implementing an executive order saying U.S. companies could only host Chinese models if they could guarantee security and take liability if it were breached, the source added.Commerce last summer also circulated draft rules within the administration leveraging its authorities to secure domestic supply chains to target Chinese open-source models, another source close to the administration said.
sentences.zh: 幕后：一位与政府关系密切的消息人士告诉 Axios，商务部去年考虑将多个中国人工智能实验室添加到其“实体名单”中，这将有效地切断美国在没有许可证的情况下的访问。消息人士称，国家安全局和白宫国家网络主任办公室去年也考虑发布有关中国人工智能实验室威胁的咨询，这实际上阻止了美国公司使用其技术。白宫考虑实施一项行政命令，规定美国公司只有在可以的情况下才能托管中国模型消息人士补充说，确保安全并在违反时承担责任。 另一位与政府关系密切的消息人士称，商务部去年夏天还在政府内部散发了规则草案，利用其权力来确保国内供应链的安全，以针对中国的开源模式。
